title: Charred Silky Kenyan Dish Bake
description: A traditional Kenyan dish featuring charred sweet potatoes and silky coconut milk, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit (220 degrees Celsius).
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the sweet potatoes in a single layer.
Drizzle with 1 tablespoon of the vegetable oil and sprinkle with salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper.
Toss the sweet potatoes to coat evenly and roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until slightly charred.
While the sweet potatoes are roasting, prepare the brined then dried chicken thighs by patting them dry with paper towels and seasoning with salt and pepper.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken thighs to the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned and cooked through.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for an additional 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and slightly charred.
In a blender or food processor, combine the coconut milk, mirin, lime juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Blend until smooth and creamy, then set aside.
To assemble the dish, arrange the roasted sweet potatoes on a serving platter or individual plates, then top with the baked chicken thighs and spoon the silky coconut milk sauce over the top.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
